back: The CAP Theorem states that a distributed data store can only provide two out of the following three guarantees at the same time: Consistency (every read receives the most recent write), Availability (every request receives a response), and Partition Tolerance (the system continues to operate despite network partitions).

front: Define and contrast the two main types of consistency models: strong consistency and eventual consistency.
back: Strong Consistency guarantees that after an update, all subsequent reads will see the updated value. It provides a single, up-to-date copy of the data. Eventual Consistency guarantees that if no new updates are made, eventually all accesses will return the last updated value. It offers higher availability and lower latency but allows for temporary inconsistencies.

front: What is a distributed consensus algorithm? Name and briefly describe one prominent example.
back: A distributed consensus algorithm is a process used to achieve agreement on a single data value among distributed processes or systems. An example is the Raft algorithm, which elects a leader who manages log replication to other servers to ensure all nodes agree on the state of the system, even if some nodes fail.

front: What is the primary goal of a distributed commit protocol like Two-Phase Commit (2PC)?
back: The primary goal of a distributed commit protocol is to ensure atomicity (all-or-nothing property) for transactions that span multiple distributed nodes or databases. 2PC achieves this by using a coordinator to first prepare all participants and then, if all agree, commit the transaction.

front: What is a Byzantine fault in a distributed system, and what is a protocol designed to handle it?
back: A Byzantine fault is a condition where a component (node) behaves arbitrarily, potentially sending malicious or conflicting messages to other nodes. Byzantine Fault Tolerance (BFT) protocols, like Practical Byzantine Fault Tolerance (PBFT), are designed to reach consensus even when some nodes act maliciously.
